old_claim.note: Construction provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains from construction to interchangeable_parts because the scoped industrial method is supported by precision machinery and armory manufacturing practice, not generic monumental construction.

source_shape.source_locator: NPS describes Hall's tooling of workshops, precision machinery, drilling machines, and a straight-cutting machine for rifles with interchangeable parts.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies specialized precision machinery and armory workshop practice as the relevant substrate.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Generic construction could not distinguish the industrial interchangeable-parts mechanism from unrelated building or infrastructure traditions.

ontology_before: Interchangeable Parts inherited a broad Classical construction prerequisite unrelated to the scoped manufacturing mechanism.
ontology_after: Interchangeable Parts is anchored to precision machinery rather than generic building capability.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: the broad construction edge was removed.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to preindustrial modular construction rather than industrial component manufacture.
A source showed monumental construction directly supplied the tolerances, gauges, or machine tools used in armory interchangeable manufacture.
A separate construction-component standardization node were introduced and made this broad edge precise.
short_reason: Construction was a generic inherited edge and did not support the industrial manufacturing scope.
